source_pr.title: planner: copy columnInfo for doubleReader case
source_pr.body:

### What problem does this PR solve?

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->


symptoms: IndexLookup double-reader plan reused the same `ColumnInfo` slice between IndexScan and TableScan, leading to aliasing and potential races.
failure_signature: IndexScan.Columns and TableScan.Columns alias in double-read (planner/core/find_best_task.go)
root_cause_explanation: In IndexLookup (double-reader) plans, the planner constructed both an IndexScan and a TableScan using the same underlying `ds.Columns` slice. Because `ColumnInfo` slices are mutable and may be adjusted during planning/optimization, sharing the same slice between two plan nodes can cause unintended cross-mutation and race conditions (especially visible under `race = "on"`). This PR fixes the issue by cloning the column info list (`util.CloneColInfos(ds.Columns)`) when building the TableScan/IndexScan in the double-read path, and adds a regression test that asserts the two plan nodes do not share the same `Columns` slice.
fix_pattern: Avoid sharing mutable slices/struct pointers between plan nodes; clone `ColumnInfo` lists (or other shared mutable state) when constructing multiple readers in IndexLookup/double-read plans.

evidence: evidence: race = "on", | stmt, err := parser.New().ParseOneStmt("select * from t use index(b) where b > 1 order by b limit 1", "", "") (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go)

files: planner/core/find_best_task.go, planner/core/issuetest/BUILD.bazel, planner/core/issuetest/main_test.go, planner/core/issuetest/planner_issue_test.go, planner/core/mock.go, planner/core/physical_plan_test.go
